120333.explanation: Turnout at municipal council elections is already limited and often even lower at referenda. Therefore, referenda do not provide a representative picture of the opinion of all residents. For weighing complex and important decisions, the municipal council is elected.

120337.explanation: It is good that residents generate sustainable energy together, but the municipality does not need to support this with extra funds. It is better to assist initiatives with space and clear rules.

120338.explanation: In our polder, we have no room for these boxes where often only migrant workers are employed.

120339.explanation: More greenery is important, but a mandatory one-to-one replanting rule is too rigid. The municipality should allow room for customization instead of extra regulations.

120342.explanation: A fireworks ban changes how New Year's Eve is celebrated. The municipality can support local initiatives and joint activities.

120343.explanation: The Political Union wants to make the waste disposal site freely accessible; this lowers the threshold to bring waste, thereby reducing littering.

120346.explanation: We want sports to be free for all residents up to age 16! No more distinctions or barriers to participate in sports!

120349.explanation: Culture and music are important for children. Through the participation scheme, children from lower-income families can already receive support. This works better than a general subsidy for all children.

120350.explanation: Sports clubs are important for society. If there are problems, the municipality can think along or provide temporary help, but financial support should not become automatic.

120351.explanation: BOS officers (special enforcement officers) should primarily focus on safety and nuisance in public spaces. It is undesirable for them to be deployed to monitor volunteers or minor issues at events.

120352.explanation: We want more camera surveillance on main roads so that when an incident occurs, we can trace where the offender went.

120354.explanation: BOS officers play an important role in supervision and safety but are not police. Additional armament is not automatically appropriate; collaboration and clear tasks are more important.

120359.explanation: Those who receive support make a meaningful contribution to the community.

120360.explanation: We support a responsible debt waiver policy.

120364.explanation: Our expansive polder offers good opportunities in some areas for smoother traffic flow.

120367.explanation: Healthcare is a general provision blind to faith or belief.

120368.explanation: Quality and price are always connected and require consideration.

120369.explanation: The municipality can support and encourage existing initiatives.

120370.explanation: Where possible, we always try to keep municipal taxes as low as possible. The reality is that costs are rising for the municipality as well.

120374.explanation: Due to the severe housing shortage, the social rental market is under pressure. Structural priority for one group does not do justice to other housing seekers. The municipality must seek solutions that are fair for everyone.

120379.explanation: We support tackling nuisance through prevention. Currently, drugs are bought on the street from dealers selling soft and hard drugs, increasing the risk of addiction. For youths, drug use has become more normalized. We want to have oversight and be able to focus on prevention through the coffee shop.

120380.explanation: The Political Union chooses 70% affordable housing and 30% social rental.

120381.explanation: The housing shortage calls for impactful solutions. This also helps keep starter homes and senior apartments affordable.

120382.explanation: We want to exclude migrant workers from the town centers. For individual room rentals to friends or students, possibilities should exist.

120383.explanation: Especially new initiatives in the city center should not add extra parking pressure to the center.

120385.explanation: We have a nice arrangement along the dike, and that will remain the limit for wind turbines. There is room for small farm mills to make businesses more sustainable.

120387.explanation: Traffic safety is important, but not every polder road is the same. The maximum speed should be determined per road and not standard lowered to 60 km/h. This is a measure to be used only as a last resort when nothing else works.

120388.explanation: Migrant workers who stay longer in the Netherlands are expected to learn the language. This group does not need financial support for that.

120389.explanation: This requires an enormous investment. Additionally, it will discourage waste separation, contaminate other waste streams, or lead to more illegal dumping. Waste collection is also a municipal service to residents; requiring everyone to walk to an underground container is not seen as a service improvement.

120333.explanation: We are duly elected representatives to tackle issues. While it is essential to involve residents on important matters, we take the lead.

120336.explanation: We have clear agreements. Within those, everyone may decide, provided the earliest/latest opening and closing times are not exceeded.

120337.explanation: These projects must become self-sufficient. The municipality can support the startup phase. The municipality has a sustainability fund for this (funded by large solar projects and not by the municipality itself).

120338.explanation: If they offer good local employment, distribution centers are welcome. But we do not want block-shaped buildings that spoil our beautiful landscape!

120339.explanation: We must ensure a good balance and a beautiful environment for future generations.

120342.explanation: This is unifying and creates a level playing field. Rich and poor can enjoy the same fireworks show. A good fireworks display is a fine alternative to a national ban.

120343.explanation: We should pay for the services we use as residents. However, we encourage residents to separate waste as well as possible. So everyone controls how much they pay. Exceptional cases should qualify for debt waiver, for example, residents with a lot of medical waste.

120346.explanation: Sports are very important for the social involvement and health of this group. Therefore, we encourage it. No one should be excluded from participating in sports.

120349.explanation: Children falling under the minimum income schemes can claim this. However, all children receive broad music orientation lessons subsidized by the municipality.

120350.explanation: Not by default, but it is the backbone of our society and provides movement and connection. That cannot be translated into a monetary amount.

120351.explanation: In recent years, the number of BOS officers has already increased. We encourage evaluation to constantly align capacity with enforcement needs.

120352.explanation: Safety is extremely important and we fully commit to it. We must handle camera surveillance with utmost care. It concerns our residents’ privacy and must not be deployed unlawfully.

120354.explanation: This is a serious measure that only needs to be applied when enforcement tasks demand it. In the Noordoostpolder, this is not yet the case. However, we do support good short communication lines between enforcement officers and the police to intervene quickly when the situation requires it.

120359.explanation: It is nice if these people can contribute to society according to their capacity. That promotes their self-reliance. The municipality encourages this. But it should not become an obligation.

120360.explanation: These people actually need to feel some financial relief. The municipality also has a responsibility towards its residents in this regard.

120364.explanation: 30 km/h where necessary and 50 km/h where possible.

120367.explanation: Freedom to choose your own care provider is a valuable right in the Netherlands. Identity-based care is of significant importance.

120368.explanation: The focus is on the best appropriate help for the young person's needs. If this is more expensive, so be it, because it prevents prolonged delays at other organizations which initially seemed cheaper.

120369.explanation: Period poverty is unacceptable. The municipality ensures that tampons and sanitary pads are available, for example at food banks. This applies to those who need them.

120370.explanation: Property tax (OZB) may increase only up to the inflation rate. This keeps taxes under control. A campaign promise that taxes will decrease would not be an honest story.

120374.explanation: These are recognized refugees and we must continue to house these people in accordance with the allocation assigned by the national government.

120379.explanation: Allowing a coffeeshop normalizes drug use. That is not the example we want to set for our children, youth, and adults. We prefer to encourage a healthy lifestyle with sports, exercise, and play.

120380.explanation: As far as we are concerned, this percentage applies only to newly built neighborhoods.

120381.explanation: Up to the maximum height of church towers in the villages. This should ensure our heritage and landscape features are preserved.

120382.explanation: Due to the housing shortage, it is necessary to think creatively. Renting out individual rooms can be an example of this. However, the municipality must enforce living conditions.

120383.explanation: At peak times there is a parking problem in the center. We need to find solutions for this. That is not only about additional parking spaces but also, for example, encouraging residents who live nearby to cycle to the center.

120385.explanation: We have allowed sufficient wind turbines along our polder border. Both on the IJsselmeer and next to our dikes.

120387.explanation: The goal is for all polder roads to have an appropriate speed limit. So where necessary, we implement 60 km/h.

120388.explanation: These lessons must definitely be given and are very important. There is a responsibility here for the employers of migrant workers, who must pay for this.

120389.explanation: We only install underground waste containers where this is necessary.

120333.explanation: Municipal council members must inform themselves well from residents about current issues. Afterwards, they must make decisions in the general interest. That means informing broadly. Supporters of a political party are not customers who have more influence than the general interest.

120336.explanation: In case of undesirable developments, the municipality must be able to set well-considered frameworks.

120337.explanation: Subsidies can be very useful to get energy projects, which we all want, off the ground.

120338.explanation: Industry that fits the character of the Noordoostpolder is always welcome. In other cases, municipal council members must make a careful consideration.

120339.explanation: For windbreak rows around farms, when cutting a row, you should even plant somewhat more young trees than are cut. In nature areas, after harvesting trees, nature can take its course. More forest on agricultural land is not a desirable development.

120342.explanation: This can better be done through citizen initiatives. The municipality can provide support and assistance.

120343.explanation: That does not have to be free. If you bring your own waste to the dump, it also costs you time, fuel, and effort.

120346.explanation: That should not be a standard. Upon request, the municipality can very well contribute to this. Rules have also been agreed upon for this.

120349.explanation: Music lessons are part of the cultural sector. Barriers for young people to take music lessons must be removed in as many ways as possible. Additional funding is not always necessary for this.

120350.explanation: The municipality should not automatically provide extra money when times are tough. Sports clubs with sufficient viability should continue to exist!

120351.explanation: Our enforcement officers are not ticket writers; they primarily engage in constructive conversations about public behavior. They have these conversations in our villages but also, for example, regarding littering in rural areas.

120352.explanation: Expansion of camera surveillance is only discussable if targeted extra camera monitoring genuinely improves the safety of our residents.

120354.explanation: We also deploy enforcement officers in residential neighborhoods and village centers to address nuisance and livability issues. Bodycams will become standard equipment for the safety of enforcement officers. A baton is a good option if legally permitted.

120359.explanation: If possible, a reciprocal contribution is very important. Not from an economic perspective, but precisely because everyone capable participates in an open society.

120360.explanation: The municipality must be able to offer customized solutions. When municipal taxes push vulnerable residents further into a debt trap, a temporary solution should be provided by the municipality. Otherwise, costs for the municipality will be much higher later.

120364.explanation: For much of our built-up area, this hardly provides any safety benefits. There is also a risk of more confusion and therefore greater insecurity.

120367.explanation: Care is not dependent on any religious or philosophical belief.

120368.explanation: Despite the enormous costs of youth care, quality and waiting times are always most important. If youth receive poor or delayed help, problems worsen and it costs the municipality many times more money in the long run.

120369.explanation: Period poverty must be combated. Randomly distributing tampons and sanitary pads is not an effective measure.

120370.explanation: The CDA wants to keep property tax (OZB) as low as possible. It directly affects the purchasing power of families and households. Therefore, we support an inflation cap on the OZB. The OZB will not rise more than inflation in the coming period.

120374.explanation: The municipality must enforce the law. That means each municipality must provide a home for a number of new Dutch residents. If this is done well, it does not come at the expense of local home seekers.

120379.explanation: The CDA does not want a coffeeshop or soft drug counter in the municipality unless it is without doubt proven that such a facility reduces drug use.

120380.explanation: The CDA supports more social rental housing for all age groups. We are in favor of mixed neighborhoods, with owner-occupied, social rental, and mid-rent housing, also provided by the housing association. This should not be disrupted all at once.

120381.explanation: More residential layers in towns can be a solution.

120382.explanation: Non-independent living units more often cause nuisance. For specific target groups (student housing or labor migrants), under certain conditions, this is not the case.

120383.explanation: And there should be a good digital parking guidance system that shows how many spaces are available per parking area. People who work in the center (including civil servants) park on the outer edge of the center.

120385.explanation: Small farm wind turbines do not disturb the horizon. And they do contribute to the energy transition.

120387.explanation: Provincial (N) roads will remain 80/100 km/h. National roads (A6) will return to 130 km/h.

120388.explanation: The municipality assists in this, preferably not with money. The municipality also helps employers who employ migrants to organize this.

120389.explanation: Where there is insufficient space for wheelie bins, underground containers are the solution.

120333.explanation: People should be able to express their opinion, for example through citizen councils. In a referendum, the outcome must be implemented, which is not always feasible.

120336.explanation: In consultation, but equal for everyone.

120337.explanation: Based on ability to pay so that everyone can participate.

120338.explanation: Does not fit with our socio-economic agenda and usually many labor migrants who need to be housed work here.

120342.explanation: It is not necessary, is aligned with villages and neighborhoods, and preferably with financial contribution from society.

120343.explanation: It would be convenient, for example once a year, after registration.

120346.explanation: Sports are important for participation, health, and social contacts.

120349.explanation: A school should be allowed to receive subsidies to provide music lessons. Through the participation scheme, children of low-income parents can make use of music lessons, which we find important.

120350.explanation: Each situation must be examined to determine the cause and how the municipality can possibly help.

120351.explanation: There has been recent expansion and if it appears we need more, then we are in favor.

120352.explanation: They already exist at important locations and expansion also means action must be taken, which is currently not feasible. It can have a deterrent effect but also provides false security and infringes on privacy.

120354.explanation: Weapons do not belong with an accessible appearance.

120359.explanation: Not everyone can work; guidance towards meaningful daytime activities is important. This can also be volunteer work.

120360.explanation: Definitely not, this is a good scheme.

120364.explanation: Not standard, but in the busier neighborhoods.

120367.explanation: It is about the quality of care.

120368.explanation: Here too, it is about quality.

120369.explanation: Yes, for the people who need it.

120370.explanation: Property tax (OZB) is not high here compared to other municipalities. OZB is also used for our facilities, such as the library and theater.

120374.explanation: There must be a good distribution so that residents from the Northern Open Country (NOP) can quickly find a home and status holders do not have to wait unnecessarily long in the reception center.

120379.explanation: There should have been one already! We want a well-regulated coffee shop to gain insight into usage and better focus on prevention.

120380.explanation: We are currently still below the norm and want to work towards an average of 30%, which may mean that new neighborhoods need 40%.

120381.explanation: Especially if the Lelylijn station will come in the future. High-rise buildings would fit well around here.

120382.explanation: A landlady who rents to students would be fine; rental to labor migrants must already meet specific requirements.

120383.explanation: People can better come by bike, and there is enough parking space around the center. More parking spaces could also mean paid parking (parking garage), and we do not want that.

120385.explanation: We support the generation of green energy. Small wind turbines can help with this; tall wind turbines can cause horizon pollution.

120387.explanation: On provincial roads, 80 km/h is appropriate, and on access roads, 60 km/h is more suitable; this also requires something from our residents themselves.

120388.explanation: Employers and employment agencies must also cooperate and contribute financially.

120389.explanation: In densely populated neighborhoods, it can be a good solution; from villages, we do not know this demand.

120336.explanation: The municipality determines the maximum closing time, for example 3:00 a.m.

120338.explanation: Depends on the size of the object.

120342.explanation: Should not, but they could consider it.

120343.explanation: Yes, everyone wants that, but it must be affordable.

120349.explanation: Music lessons within the standard curriculum do not need extra subsidies. Outside school hours, this should be possible. It contributes to the development of a child.

120350.explanation: Yes, sports are essential for a village and contribute to cohesion. Assistance is of course provided under conditions.

120368.explanation: In addition, the care supply must be better aligned with the care demand.

120381.explanation: With a maximum of four floors.

120388.explanation: Yes, we must have the possibility to facilitate this due to rapid integration.

120389.explanation: It is already being collected, so not applicable.

120333.explanation: The municipal council is elected to represent the residents. When developing policy, we actively involve our residents. Therefore, a referendum is redundant and a waste of time.

120336.explanation: Hospitality and terraces contribute to a vibrant city center. This requires generous opening hours.

120337.explanation: Local initiatives to jointly generate sustainable energy can count on support in the startup phase in the form of knowledge and funding from the sustainability fund.

120338.explanation: We stand for high-quality employment. Companies that provide jobs, are embedded in society, and contribute to innovation. Distribution centers do not fit this.

120339.explanation: This is too strict a rule. We do find it important that our living environment is and remains sufficiently green. We take this into account in all developments.

120342.explanation: Fireworks are a tradition associated with New Year's Eve. We find it a shame if this tradition is lost. If residents can no longer light fireworks themselves, the municipality will organize a fireworks show.

120343.explanation: We want to keep the costs for waste collection as low as possible. Bulky waste can be brought by residents to the recycling center, often free of charge during generous opening hours.

120346.explanation: We find it important that everyone can play sports and be active. We financially support children's sports through the participation scheme for those whose parents cannot afford it. For us, that is sufficient.

120349.explanation: We consider cultural education for children important, but parents are responsible for that themselves. Through the participation scheme, we support children of parents who truly lack financial means.

120350.explanation: Sports clubs themselves are responsible for remaining financially healthy. We subsidize Carrefour to help sports clubs stay strong and future-proof.

120351.explanation: We consider safety important. With more BOAs (special enforcement officers) on the streets, more targeted enforcement and supervision can take place. This benefits livability and safety.

120352.explanation: We consider safety important. Camera surveillance increases the chance of catching offenders and ensures supervision at risk locations. It enhances safety and has a preventive effect.

120354.explanation: We consider safety important. If BOAs need more or different tools to do their work well and safely, we allow that. We think not only of batons, but also pepper spray and body cams.

120359.explanation: Residents receiving social assistance are expected to give something in return, obviously taking into account what someone can do, and with municipal support where needed. This also ensures that everyone stays involved in society.

120360.explanation: For residents in financially vulnerable situations, waiving municipal taxes is the easiest way to support them.

120364.explanation: In residential neighborhoods and places where needed, such as near schools, the maximum speed may be reduced to 30 km/h. This is already often the case.

120367.explanation: Every healthcare provider must be able to offer appropriate care to residents with different religious beliefs, cultures, or life philosophies.

120368.explanation: Youth care must remain affordable for the municipality. Therefore, the price an organization charges for youth help is an important selection criterion alongside quality.

120369.explanation: There are sufficient social organizations that take care of this.

120370.explanation: The burdens on our residents and entrepreneurs are already high enough and we keep them as low as possible. Increasing property tax (ozb) to balance the budget is unacceptable.

120374.explanation: The waiting times for social rental homes are long. Our own residents sometimes wait years for a home. It is unfair to give status holders priority.

120379.explanation: If a coffeeshop contributes to limiting illegality and nuisance around drug use and trade, we are willing to explore possibilities for establishing a coffeeshop.

120380.explanation: We find 40% of newly built homes as social rental too high. This also does not match the current and future needs of our residents.

120381.explanation: Given the high demand for housing, building must proceed quickly. Building higher can be an option to counter the housing shortage but must fit the surroundings. That is why we only allow more than 5 floors in Emmeloord.

120382.explanation: We want to prevent labor migrants from different families living in one house in Emmeloord and the villages. This has caused nuisance in the past.

120383.explanation: You should be able to drive by car to the center of Emmeloord and park there for free. There are currently insufficient parking spaces. A parking garage or parking deck can solve this.

120385.explanation: We love our open landscape. If extra wind turbines are chosen in the future to meet our local energy needs, we will place them in the IJsselmeer.

120387.explanation: Safe roads are very important to us. There must be a balance between safety and speed. Lowering the maximum speed limit often does not improve safety. 60 where necessary, 80 where possible.

120388.explanation: This is the responsibility of the employers of the labor migrants. This is part of being an employer.

120389.explanation: We find service very important in waste collection. The current method of waste collection works well and we will maintain it.

120333.explanation: A referendum can be a good tool for certain important issues that affect everyone to involve residents in decision-making. However, this option must be used carefully: the instrument should only be deployed for weighty decisions.

120336.explanation: Our municipality could use a bit more liveliness. There must be more to experience, the nightlife should offer more to our residents. Then it does not help if we as a municipality want to apply limited opening hours.

120337.explanation: A financial boost to encourage residents to work together on making our living environment more sustainable is a great idea as far as Brede Horizon is concerned. The focus should primarily be on residents who benefit most from such an incentive.

120338.explanation: Large distribution centers disrupt the open landscape for which Noordoostpolder is known. Moreover, they take up a lot of space, space we would rather use for initiatives that are more socially valuable.

120339.explanation: Noordoostpolder is not exactly known for its abundance of trees, so let’s not reduce that number any further. Furthermore, a greener living environment is needed to ensure comfortable living in the future (especially during hotter summers). More trees, therefore, not fewer.

120342.explanation: Keeping in mind the introduction of the nationwide consumer fireworks ban, Brede Horizon thinks it’s a good idea to organize central fireworks shows. Sustainable alternatives, such as drone shows, can also be considered.

120343.explanation: The polluter pays, so it is fair to charge a financial contribution for home pickup of bulky waste.

120346.explanation: Brede Horizon believes that all residents should be able to participate in sports. A limited budget should not be an obstacle. Residents receiving social assistance should also be able to join a sports club, and this applies even more to children from financially struggling households.

120349.explanation: Culture enriches and broadens your horizon. Subsidizing every primary school child to have the chance to experience this is an investment in a more diverse society. A good idea indeed!

120350.explanation: Agreed, but only to a limited extent and preferably only once. When a municipality financially steps in, there should be a process in place requiring the club concerned to get its affairs in order to prevent recurrence.

120351.explanation: Safety is an important condition for a pleasant society. Brede Horizon believes there is too little police capacity and too few enforcement officers (BOAs). It is therefore important to invest more in this.

120352.explanation: Yes, but only at strategic locations and with a clear objective. Brede Horizon thinks of main roads to prevent theft of expensive agricultural equipment, and locations in the center of Emmeloord where safety is at risk.

120354.explanation: A baton is a weapon. Brede Horizon is not in favor of this. We see more need for expanding the number of enforcement officers (BOAs) in Noordoostpolder.

120359.explanation: If the situation allows, Brede Horizon supports doing socially useful work. Not so much as a form of repayment, but mainly to involve people in our society and improve their transition to regular employment.

120360.explanation: For a small portion of our lowest-income residents, stopping debt forgiveness helps no one. On the contrary, it causes harm to households (think of families in poverty) that benefits no one.

120364.explanation: Brede Horizon agrees because this demonstrably leads to greater safety for fellow road users such as cyclists and pedestrians. Fewer accidents, fewer injuries – in short, limiting the maximum speed in built-up areas is only logical.

120367.explanation: First and foremost, the focus is on providing good care. That this is provided by someone matching their faith, culture, or beliefs is of secondary importance.

120368.explanation: When it comes to youth care, price should never be leading. Quality obviously receives the highest priority here. Vulnerable young people do not benefit from the cheapest provider.

120369.explanation: For one in eight girls and women in the Netherlands, there is no money at home for sanitary pads or tampons. Hygiene is not a luxury but a basic need that must be accessible to everyone.

120370.explanation: Property tax (OZB) should definitely not increase. Brede Horizon sees that Noordoostpolder is financially in excellent shape, so we are not in favor of raising the OZB. A reduction is not necessary: in our view, maintaining the current level is reasonable.

120374.explanation: Yes, because these are residents allowed to stay in the Netherlands. Both these people themselves and our society benefit from the integration process going as smoothly as possible. Having one’s own home helps. The focus should be on more housing construction; that solves these types of dilemmas.

120379.explanation: Under certain conditions, it might be conceivable for us as a municipality to permit the establishment of a coffeeshop. This must, however, be done based on careful policy.

120380.explanation: Brede Horizon firmly believes that the number of social rental homes must increase in both Emmeloord and the villages. The waiting lists for rental homes have risen to an unacceptable level in recent years. This development must be reversed as soon as possible.

120381.explanation: Yes, but specifically in Emmeloord and based on well-considered spatial planning. High residential towers don’t fit in the villages, but Brede Horizon sees space for more apartment buildings there. This way, we create room for more affordable housing for young and old.

120382.explanation: Brede Horizon chooses to allow limited single-room rentals because it contributes to affordable housing for young people and starters. At the same time, we set strict and clear rules to protect livability, safety, and cohesion in neighborhoods.

120383.explanation: Parking space in the center of Emmeloord is a problem, especially on certain days and peak times. Brede Horizon supports building an (underground) parking garage. De Deel would be a good location for this: the area can then be attractively designed at the same time.

120385.explanation: Yes, but with an important condition: only small wind turbines that do not disrupt the landscape are allowed. Brede Horizon is pleased that wind turbines are not randomly placed everywhere in Noordoostpolder; this way, we handle the special character of our area carefully.

120387.explanation: Brede Horizon believes there is a distinction between through roads (mostly provincial) and smaller, non-through access roads. For the latter category, 60 km/h is a sensible speed for safety reasons (considering fellow road users such as cyclists).

120388.explanation: As a municipality and society, we benefit if labor migrants who settle permanently in Noordoostpolder learn the Dutch language well as quickly as possible.

120389.explanation: This would require a major investment by the municipality, and it is questionable whether it is worth it. Underground containers near high-rise buildings and apartment complexes, however, is a good idea.

statements.title: There should be underground containers for residual waste in all villages.

titleParts.text: In alle dorpen moeten ondergrondse containers voor

titleParts.glossary: Waste that remains after paper, plastic and organic waste have been collected separately.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The residents of Noordoostpolder should be able to express their opinions on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Noordoostpolder moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: At least 40% of the new homes in Noordoostpolder should be social rental housing. Currently, 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Noordoostpolder moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) in Noordoostpolder who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Noordoostpolder.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should subsidize Dutch language lessons for migrant workers.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: geven voor lessen in Nederlandse taal aan

titleParts.glossary: Foreign workers.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
There should be more parking spaces in the center of Emmeloord.
The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should allow wind turbines on land.

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: Noordoostpolder should allow more high-rise residential buildings.
The BOAs who enforce safety in Noordoostpolder should be issued a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Noordoostpolder controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The maximum speed on the polder roads should be reduced to 60 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.

titleParts.text: op de polderwegen moet naar 60 km/u.
